basicDetails.schemeName: Bharati Scheme for Education - Intermediate or Equivalent

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The "Bharati Scheme for Education - Intermediate or Equivalent (ITI, Polytechnic, D.Ed, TTC, D.Pharmacy, etc.)" introduced by the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation aims to encourage Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Under this scheme, eligible poor Brahmin student applicants will receive financial assistance for the academic year. This scheme applies to the Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh, covering all 13 districts. The Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation implements the scheme.
schemeContent.benefits_md:
1. Financial Assistance: ₹10,000/-. 1. Intended Use: To encourage Brahmin students to continue their education.
> **Mode of Disbursement:** The amount of Financial Assistance granted will be credited through online to the SB Accounts of the students.
schemeContent.exclusions_md: The applicant should not have availed himself/herself any amount under any other Government scheme for the same purpose.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided to eligible poor Brahmin students.

children.text: The "Bharati Scheme for Education - Intermediate or Equivalent (ITI, Polytechnic, D.Ed, TTC, D.Pharmacy, etc.)" introduced by the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation aims to encourage Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Under this scheme, eligible poor Brahmin student applicants will receive financial assistance for the academic year. This scheme applies to the Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh, covering all 13 districts. The Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation implements the scheme.
Intended Use: To encourage Brahmin students to continue their education.
The amount of Financial Assistance granted will be credited through online to the SB Accounts of the students.
The applicant should not have availed himself/herself any amount under any other Government scheme for the same purpose.
Click on "Registration" under the concerned scheme name.
Fill out the application form with required details like:
Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etc
Upload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details
Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etc
Receive the reference number after the submission.
Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "
Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status.
Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details.
Click on the "Acknowledgement Slip" under the concerned scheme.
Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme".

applicationProcess.process_md:
> **Registration** **Step-01:** Applicants have to visit the [official website](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/) and click on [Scheme](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#). **Step-02:** Click on "Registration" under the concerned scheme name. **Step-03:** Fill out the application form with required details like: - Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etc - Upload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details - Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etc - Finalize and "Submit" the application. **Step-04:** Receive the reference number after the submission.
> **Check Application Status:** **Step-01:** Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "[Know your status](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/eSchemes/know_stat.aspx)". **Step-02:** Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status. **Step-03:** Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details.
> **Download the Acknowledgement slip:** **Step-01:** Applicants have to visit the [official website](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/) and click on [Scheme](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#). **Step-02:** Click on the "Acknowledgement Slip" under the concerned scheme. **Step-03:** Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme". **Step-04:** Click on the "Submit".

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The student and his/her parents should belong to the Brahmin community. 1. The parents should reside in Andhra Pradesh. 1. The student's parents/guardians should stay in Andhra Pradesh. 1. The student should be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey (PSS). 1. The annual family income of Parents/guardians should not be more than ₹3,00,000/- (Rupees Three lakhs only). 1. The student should be pursuing a regular course in a School/College/Institute/University for the Academic year 2019-20. 1. The student should have passed in all the subjects till the previous year of study (i.e., students who do not have any backlogs in their previous semesters/years are only eligible). 1. The students availing the benefit from Archaka Welfare Trust are eligible under this scheme, in addition to the assistance they get from Archaka Welfare Trust.

children.text: The student and his/her parents should belong to the Brahmin community.
The parents should reside in Andhra Pradesh.
The student's parents/guardians should stay in Andhra Pradesh.
The student should be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey (PSS).
The annual family income of Parents/guardians should not be more than ₹3,00,000/- (Rupees Three lakhs only).
The student should be pursuing a regular course in a School/College/Institute/University for the Academic year 2019-20.
The student should have passed in all the subjects till the previous year of study (i.e., students who do not have any backlogs in their previous semesters/years are only eligible).
The students availing the benefit from Archaka Welfare Trust are eligible under this scheme, in addition to the assistance they get from Archaka Welfare Trust.
